name: Cefadroxil
indication: For the treatment of the following infections (skin, UTI, ENT) caused by; S. pneumoniae, H. influenzae, staphylococci, S. pyogenes (group A beta-hemolytic streptococci), E. coli, P. mirabilis, Klebsiella sp, coagulase-negative staphylococci and Streptococcus pyogenes
toxicity: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, allergic rashes may occur
mechanismOfAction: Like all beta-lactam antibiotics, cefadroxil binds to specific penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located inside the bacterial cell wall, causing the inhibition of the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. Cell lysis is then mediated by bacterial cell wall autolytic enzymes such as autolysins; it is possible that cefadroxil interferes with an autolysin inhibitor.
pharmacodynamics: Cefadroxil, a first-generation cephalosporin antibiotic, is used to treat urinary tract infections, skin and skin structure infections, pharyngitis, and tonsillitis.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefadroxil.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefadroxil.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefadroxil may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefadroxil can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
